analysis_result: 7/8 MPNST-G1 tumors show 8q gain (87.5%) using a Python methylprep-based CONUMEE substitute (log2 total-intensity ratio vs 20 Benign_NF-G4 reference samples, arm-level threshold > +0.10).
denominator_used: N = 8 MPNST_G1 tumors as defined by Methylation_Group in Supplementary Data 1 (samples: M1014, M2252, M2346, M2372, M3048, M3398, M6712, M803).
assumptions_made: CONUMEE R package unavailable, so used Python methylprep log2-ratio substitute as instructed; total CpG intensity = Grn+Red per address (Type II) or AddressA+AddressB in color channel (Type I); per-sample centering of autosomal median log2 intensity; reference = 20 Benign_NF-G4 samples (median across reference is the normal); 8q defined as chr8 CpGs with MAPINFO > 45.2 Mb (hg19 centromere); arm-level summary = trimmed mean (5-95 percentile) of CpG log2 ratios; gain threshold = log2 ratio > +0.10; no explicit segmentation (CBS) performed, only arm-level aggregation; hg19 genome build assumed for manifest MAPINFO.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx: 108 samples clinical metadata including Tumor_ID, Methylation_Group, Sentrix_ID.
   - GSE207207_idats/: 216 IDAT files (108 samples x Grn/Red).
   - GPL21145_MethylationEPIC_15073387_v-1-0.csv.gz: EPIC manifest (864,935 CpG probes) with IlmnID, AddressA_ID, AddressB_ID, Infinium_Design_Type, Color_Channel, CHR, MAPINFO.

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - Filter: Methylation_Group == 'MPNST_G1' -> 8 samples (M1014, M2252, M2346, M2372, M3048, M3398, M6712, M803).
   - Reference: Methylation_Group == 'Benign_NF-G4' -> 20 samples.
   - Backend: R/Bioconductor conumee not installed; used Python methylprep as instructed fallback.

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - Total CpG intensity: Type II probes: grn[AddressA] + red[AddressA]; Type I probes: intensity[AddressA] + intensity[AddressB] in the Color_Channel's channel.
   - 8q probes: CHR == '8' AND MAPINFO > 45,200,000 bp (hg19 centromere position). n = 24,144 CpGs.

D. COMPUTATION
   - For each of 28 samples (8 MPNST_G1 + 20 Benign_NF-G4), parsed IDAT files with methylprep.IdatDataset and built total CpG intensity vector (864,935 CpGs).
   - log2 intensity per CpG per sample.
   - Centered each sample so autosomal median log2 intensity = 0.
   - Reference profile = median of centered log2 intensity across 20 Benign_NF-G4 samples, per CpG.
   - log2 ratio = sample log2 intensity (centered) - reference log2 intensity.
   - Arm-level 8q log2 ratio = trimmed mean (5-95 percentile) of log2 ratios across 24,144 CpGs on 8q.
   - Gain call: arm-level log2 ratio > +0.10.

E. RESULT
   Per-sample 8q arm log2 ratios (MPNST_G1):
     M1014:  +0.2915 GAIN
     M2252:  +0.2004 GAIN
     M2346:  +0.1420 GAIN
     M2372:  +0.1790 GAIN
     M3048:  +0.0368 (no gain)
     M3398:  +0.3085 GAIN
     M6712:  +0.2607 GAIN
     M803:   +0.1201 GAIN
   
   8q gain frequency in MPNST_G1: 7/8 (87.5%).
   
   Sanity check - Benign_NF-G4 reference distribution: n=20, min=-0.077, median=-0.003, max=+0.101, SD=0.041; all but one reference sample below the +0.10 threshold, confirming the arm-level threshold discriminates tumor from normal.

F. CAVEATS
   - CONUMEE R package unavailable -- this is a Python methylprep log2-ratio substitute as specified in the question's fallback instruction (benign NFs as reference, arm-level threshold ~+0.1).
   - No circular binary segmentation (CBS) was performed; calls are based on arm-level trimmed mean rather than per-segment CONUMEE log2 ratios. True CONUMEE may call focal 8q gain in some samples that appear globally neutral at arm level, or vice versa.
   - hg19 centromere position (45.2 Mb for chr8) used; EPIC v1 manifest MAPINFO is hg19-aligned so this is consistent.
   - One MPNST_G1 sample (M3048) has borderline 8q log2 ratio (+0.04) overlapping the reference distribution; this is the only non-gain call and would not be called gain at any standard threshold.
   - Reference normalization is per-CpG median across 20 Benign_NF-G4 samples, which matches the principle of CONUMEE's control panel.
